title: rosa canina var. hispanica
intro: Rosa canina var. hispanica, also known as the dwarf dog rose or Spanish rose, is a shrub species of flowering plant that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a variant of Rosa canina, which is a rose species that has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. hispanica include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be made from its flowers, leaves, and hips (seed pods) to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
